title: Multi-Modal Fusion Transformer for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
authors: Aditya Prakash, Kashyap Chitta, Andreas Geiger
summary: A representative early end-to-end driving model that fuses camera and LiDAR features with transformers. Useful as background for the fast reactive expert in dual-expert driving systems.

title: Planning-Oriented Autonomous Driving
summary: A landmark planning-oriented end-to-end framework that unifies perception, prediction, and planning. It is a key reference when positioning modern end-to-end autonomous driving pipelines.

title: Think Twice Before Driving: Towards Scalable Decoders for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
summary: Introduces a scalable decoder with coarse-to-fine refinement for end-to-end driving. It is relevant as an example of adaptive computation inside an end-to-end policy, even though it is not a dual-expert switcher.

title: DriveAdapter: Breaking the Coupling Barrier of Perception and Planning in End-to-End Autonomous Driving
summary: A strong end-to-end driving framework that explicitly revisits the coupling between perception and planning. It is useful for discussing how end-to-end systems improve robustness without moving to large multimodal reasoning models.

title: VAD: Vectorized Scene Representation for Efficient Autonomous Driving
summary: A representative efficient end-to-end method that replaces dense rasterized BEV with vectorized scene representation. It is especially relevant when motivating compact fast experts for deployment.

title: Navigation-Guided Sparse Scene Representation for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
summary: A sparse end-to-end driving framework that uses a compact navigation-guided representation and minimizes dependence on explicit perception supervision. It is directly relevant as a fast expert candidate for Triggered-VLA.

title: DriveLM: Driving with Graph Visual Question Answering
summary: A representative VLM-style autonomous driving work that formulates reasoning with graph VQA and supports interactive scene understanding. It is a key citation when introducing language-grounded reasoning in driving.

title: Making Large Language Models Better Planners with Reasoning-Decision Alignment
summary: An ECCV 2024 language-grounded planning method that aligns intermediate reasoning with driving decisions. It is useful when discussing how language reasoning is made behavior-relevant rather than purely descriptive.

title: DriveVLM: The Convergence of Autonomous Driving and Large Vision-Language Models
summary: Introduces a VLM-based driving system and a hybrid DriveVLM-Dual variant that combines a slow reasoning branch with a traditional driving branch. This makes it one of the closest precursors to dual-expert autonomous driving.

title: Continuously Learning, Adapting, and Improving: A Dual-Process Approach to Autonomous Driving
summary: A dual-process autonomous driving framework inspired by System-1 and System-2 cognition. It is relevant to two-branch design because it explicitly separates heuristic driving from analytic reasoning.

title: DriveGPT4: Interpretable End-to-End Autonomous Driving via Large Language Model
summary: An early influential LLM-for-driving work that couples language-based explanation with end-to-end control prediction. It is helpful for framing the interpretability and reasoning motivation behind later VLA systems.

title: DriveGPT4-V2: Harnessing Large Language Model Capabilities for Enhanced Closed-Loop Autonomous Driving
summary: Extends the DriveGPT line toward stronger closed-loop driving with improved tokenization, decision prediction, and inference speed. It is a representative recent large-model driving baseline.

title: S4-Driver: Scalable Self-Supervised Driving Multimodal Large Language Model with Spatio-Temporal Visual Representation
summary: A multimodal large-model driving method that transfers strong visual-language priors into 3D spatio-temporal planning without relying on dense manual annotations. It is useful when discussing the scaling trend of VLA-style driving models.

title: SimLingo: Vision-Only Closed-Loop Autonomous Driving with Language-Action Alignment
summary: A vision-language-action model that jointly supports driving, VQA, and language-action alignment. It is a strong recent reference for unified VLA-style closed-loop driving.

title: AdaDrive: Self-Adaptive Slow-Fast System for Language-Grounded Autonomous Driving
summary: A slow-fast language-grounded driving framework that adapts large-model assistance according to scene complexity and prediction confidence. It is directly relevant to both dual-branch design and branch activation policy.

title: ETA: Efficiency through Thinking Ahead, A Dual Approach to Self-Driving with Large Models
summary: An asynchronous dual-system architecture that combines a small online model with heavy large-model reasoning shifted to earlier time steps. It is highly relevant when discussing efficient deployment of dual-expert driving systems.

title: AdaThinkDrive: Adaptive Thinking via Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Driving
summary: A recent adaptive-reasoning VLA framework with fast-answer and slow-thinking modes. Although not yet a main-conference paper, it is directly relevant to branch switching and reasoning-on-demand in driving.
